title: Remove Connections/Links in Connect Boards Column if Linked item(s) were deleted
excerpt:
Hello Community 
What is meant to happen when I have an item in a board, to which another board in another item is linked (via a ‘connect boards’ relationship) and that item is deleted? sometimes i notice that the link t…

posts.author: jaacquees (Jacques Barouhiel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Community

What is meant to happen when I have an item in a board, to which another board in another item is linked (via a ‘connect boards’ relationship) and that item is deleted? sometimes i notice that the link to the deleted item is still present in the other board, causing some unwanted effect (incorrect summations in mirror columns, etc.)

I know deleted items are meant to be rare, but it is hard to prevent all our users from deleting items (e.g. when mistakes are made, etc.) Is there a way to gracefully unlink an item as it is being deleted?

Failing that, are there any plans to add a webhook (or a recipe trigger) for when a user deletes an item?

posts.author: Gil (Gil S T)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I think I’m having the same issue.
I recently noticed that when I delete an item which is referenced elsewhere (e.g. has an item linked to it or dependent on it), the reference stays (even though the item no longer exists and cannot be accessed for editing).
In my case I needed to move items to a different board as part of restructuring my board set-up. I thought the easiest way to do that would be to delete the original items (assuming the references would be deleted) and then recreating the references to the new location. However, since the references did not get deleted I had to manually remove them and recreate everything (for more than 100 items…).

posts.author: IanSu (IanSu)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree with you guys.

I think the expected result is that these item-links are also deleted when the item itself is deleted.

posts.author: SRudorf (Sandra Rudorf)
posts.cookedHtml: …same problem here!

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

Thank you for all of your feedback here. I too was able to reproduce this issue!

As such, I would be more than happy to forward your feedback to our Product team so that they may take this into consideration for future iterations of our product.

-Helen

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
monday.monday.com

Welcome to monday.com | a new way of working

monday.com Work OS is an open platform where anyone can create the tools they need to run every aspect of their work.

Cheese post

posts.author: Lucia1 (Lucia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello! Is there any update on this? This is a critical issue because some boards have hundreds of items and we cannot manually delete all the links one by one… We deleted the items permanently but monday keeps storing them somewhere else, so they still apear (and can be edited) in the ‘connect boards’ column

posts.author: DenstadConsulting (Thomas Denstad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Helen
Any updates on this?
It is extremely dangerous to have these dead links still being visible after the item i deleted.
We are running festivals and concert venues, and many departments will not have updated information if the links are not removed automatically.
It is sad to see that I have to send out explanations to users with a check list just for deleting an item.

Please, please have someone look into this.

Thanks
Thomas

posts.author: george_planit (George)
posts.cookedHtml: hi, I have also experienced this issue, and am disappointed that such a thing exists. I just rebuilt our boards to use mirrored items extensively, and now I have a bunch of ‘dead’ items putting wrong data into our boards. What is the workaround here?

posts.author: Mischmaster (Michael Luber)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

same problem here. Please solve it. It will get really chaotic, if deleted items are still linked!

Michael

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Lucia1 , @DenstadConsulting , @george_planit @Mischmaster –

While we cannot provide an update regarding this suggestion, as Helen mentioned, I can confirm that your feedback has all been passed on to our Product Team for future improvements! I’ve also added in all additional comments since Helen’s message to show the impact of adding this adjustment to the connect boards column.

Our Product Team takes user feedback very seriously! In fact, many of our features began as user requests.

While I know this isn’t the exact update you were hoping for, I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: arthur.bershadskiy (Arthur Bershadskiy)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jennap

Just wanted to throw in another vote for this type of request. Considering that the workflow often ends up requiring duplicating an item in a new board and connecting them, it is really difficult to have to manage deletions in multiple places. If an item is connected to another board and the original item is deleted, it should definitely delete the copy otherwise there is a lot of duplicate work required.

posts.author: colin.ellis (Colin)
posts.cookedHtml: Also putting in my vote to have this resolved as we’ve noticed it happening on our end.

posts.author: MatthieuSK (Matthieu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update please?
Sorry, but it is just a critical issue (data is not reliable due to this kind of bug)

posts.author: Mischmaster (Michael Luber)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes… please fix that.

posts.author: DenstadConsulting (Thomas Denstad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just a reminder:
In one week, this thread has been sitting here for 1 year…1 year, and still nothing.

posts.author: rbichener (Ross Bichener)
posts.cookedHtml: Would also like this to be fixed please

posts.author: tom.brink (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have come across this post after attempting to do very similar processes, just to realize it doesn’t work as expected.

I am trying to set up multiple connected boards and am noticing that the linked connections do not seem to delete when the pulse is removed from a connected board (I have refreshed the board as well as let it sit for a couple of days and refresh again). I have also noticed that if I move a pulse from one connected board to another connected board, the link does not move to the corresponding column. It is as if the initial link is created, but they are not dynamic to update these changes in the connected board.

My attempted use case might be more than the intended use of the feature. I will attempt to explain:
My workspace is for a creative environment, so we receive leads that go through reviews before proceeding on to a production process. Once a lead is vetted, it moves to either Board A or Board B (depending on the team (skillset) to do the job). And I have one more board for tracking Client details.

All 4 of these boards are connected (Clients, Leads, Board A, and Board B) with 2-way communication. From my Leads board I connect to the client for a simple link to show more client info. (I have no problems with this.) My issue arises from the connections from Leads, Board A, and Board B to my Client board (which has columns “Current Leads”, “Current Board A”, and “Current Board B”). When a lead is created on the Lead board, it is properly linked to from the Client board, in the “Current Leads” column. When a lead is vetted and moved to either Board A or Board B (depending on job skillset), my Client board does not update with a link in the corresponding column, and the link remains in the “Current Leads” column even though the pulse has been moved to the other board and removed from Leads (by automation).

From the Clients board, if I go to the “Current Leads” cell for the applicable client and click the + sign, Monday accurately does not show the linked pulse as an option for that cell. Likewise, if I click the + sign in the applicable “Board A” or “Board B” cell, the pulse is listed there as an option.

If this would work, it would be very beneficial to go to my Clients board and quickly have links to any works that are currently in production. Eventually, I would like to also create columns that link to all of their past productions that have been completed.

Please address this issue.

posts.author: RaoulVega (R. Vega)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just had to realize, that my whole board concept ain‘t working because of that issue. This issue makes the mirroring concept obsolete, in too many ways. Makes me even wondering, how monday.com can advertise this „mirroring“ feature.

Is there any improvement in work?

posts.author: AdamW (Adam Walton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am new to Monday.com and building a revenue analytics application. I am shocked that a) this fault exists in the first place and b) after more than a year it has not been resolved. I cannot go live with the application while this fault is unresolved as it will undermine the integrity of data on which decision-makers need to rely. I am questioning my choice of Monday.com as the platform for this application.

Monday.com Product Team, please provide a clear statement of intent on resolving this.

posts.author: Syracus (Syracus)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a complete dealbreaker: I joined monday.com to use it as CRM for donations, not knowing in my trial period, that this issue exist. In their own donations-CRM-Template this issue happens. So imagine if you deal with donations every such mistake can lead to big problems. Regretting my decision to move monday.com as CRM as of now. Hope this problem - that should have never existed - resolves asap. But I have no hopes, since this problem exists for over a year and nothing has been done. I agree:

monday.com Product Team, please provide a clear statement of intent on resolving this or otherwise I have to change the CRM.

posts.author: Alex_AW (Alex A. Wood)

But don’t expect this short term, since this is over a year overdue and nothing moves in that direction - and future stranded people, please vote on it, so that it get’s heard once upon a time…upon then: I bid you goodbye.

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: We gather here for a sad celebration of about 1 year, 6 months since this bug has been first reported. Sadly nothing has been done so far. Please vote (on the top left) on that issue. I am sure that many many people have that same critical problem, but don’t act on it, so the developers don’t see it as a big deal. Let’s make this seen by more people and get more votes on it.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone

Created a temporary fix which you can download here. Basically, you select a connected column and the app will find and remove any deleted item from that column. Hope monday.com will fix this natively in the future

posts.author: Kofi (Kofi FiftyTechSolutions.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @kolaai has anyone been able to confirm this consistently works? I want to try use this for a few clients.

posts.author: carolinem (Caroline McKinnon)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my vote. This urgently needs fixing.

posts.author: Khang (Khang Pham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bumping this thread.

Any Update here? @jennap

posts.author: JackHeadford (Jack Headford)
posts.cookedHtml:
YES. You hero.

Can confirm this works @Kofi, so satisfying to open up Split View and watch them get deleted after almost a year of waiting for this fix… any chance of setting this up automatically @kolaai to run once a day at 9am for instance?

posts.author: carolinem (Caroline McKinnon)
posts.cookedHtml: Interesting - this didn’t work for me. I wonder what you did differently?

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @kolaai ! The workaround is a huge help and does work in general and is a life-boat for all here stranded. I hope our data is safe and secure though, since the app can read it all.

But @carolinem there is not all done and fixed here:

  1. For example to get the working fix you always have to manually push an extra reload on your whole board-list so that the app can do it’s correction-thing. If you don’t reload it extra, it shows the old wrong calculations and produces the same errors (the deleted entries and such are back).
  2. When I export my board-list, it does not take the corrected data. It takes the original data, so it’s sadly not usable for us, because the export is what matters in the end. This is very tricky.

It’s absolutely not clear to me, why monday does not fix this bug natively for years now! It is clearly not a feature request here, it is a bug. It is such an important problem and would fix all CRM-usage problems in one fix.

Please correct this natively monday, so that we can finally properly use your app. The problem is not gone at all. Thanks.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone ,
Happy to hear that you are having success with the app.
@JackHeadford Concerning the automatic run, unfortunately I don’t see that happening since that will be too much work for something that is supposed to be a temporary fix.

@carolinem Sorry to hear that it isn’t working for you. I actually tested app multiple times and it works but I realized that, in some instances, the app correctly asks monday to remove the items but monday still keeps them in the board. Anytime you see the ‘Column fixed’ success message, it means that the app found some deleted or archived items and removed them. If they still remain, it’s because monday is not removing them even though the app asked it to. For that, there is nothing we can do about it. It’s a bug that monday has to fix. This can be liken to changing a status label from let’s say “Working on it” to “Done” but monday not changing it as you requested.

@Tomday Concerning your data, the app does not send any data outside of your monday. Everything is processed right in your browser so your data is safe.

Hope this helps

Lastly, if you want to easily copy and paste columns from Excel, Google sheets, Word etc. directly into your monday columns, you might want to check out Toolkit. It does this and many more.

Also, if you want to handle duplicates, update existing items, move form responses to subitems, create dynamic subitem templates, create subitems at the click of a button and many more automatically, you might want to check out the Duplicates and Uniques app. It’s a valuable tool to have in your account.

If you have any more questions, you can always reach out at support@kolaai.com
Have a wonderful day

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: It is hard to understand why this (remove connection when linked item is removed) is not solved. Monday introduced a whole new range of webhooks (that include item deletion trigger) so other apps like Rollup Multiple Boards) do indeed remove the connetcion when the linked item is removed.

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
@basdebruin Absolutely! It makes no sense at all. To me adverts like this: https://monday.com/blog/nonprofit/donor-management-software/ are actually really false, cause in real life and use you can’t work with that template (where money is involved) without the option of “remove connection when linked item is removed”. As it is it would mean that you would never ever ever make a mistake in your donor management…

So my point is, this one single fix would make all those Templates very valuable and for the first time actually usable. It is for that reason that I can’t understand the lack of interest from monday to fix this fast. They would get a ton of happy new customers.

posts.author: Mark_A (Mark Addison)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am having the same issue with mysterious connected items from boards that have since been deleted. As others have said, this is a data integrity issue and materially impacts the expected functionality and benefit of the connect boards feature. Therefore, this is not a debate about priority and community up-voting, it should be a bugfix marked with very high priority within Monday.com.

After discussion with the support team today, I have been informed a fix is planned for 28th September 2022 and we can expect an announcement here (or in the main community channel) ASAP.

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: @Mark_A So, did anything happen in that regard? I would be surprised if this would be fixed.

posts.author: Mark_A (Mark Addison)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Tomday - Monday.com have informed me they are progressively rolling it from 28th Sept through to end of Oct, starting with 5% of customers. So some customers should already see this fix (is anyone in this thread one of them?)

I am not sure why Monday.com steadfastly refuse to respond to this topic publicly and provide more details.

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: @Mark_A I tested this on my system and there is (no surprise there) no change. If end of Oct 5% of customers have that I would be extremely positive surprised and there is no way to know that. Sounds to me, they stalled you and sent you on a goose-chase on this one. Welcome to the wait, it only has been two years…

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone, Orit here from monday.
Yesterday we disconnected all the deleted and archived boards from ‘Connect Boards’ & ‘Mirror’ columns in all the active boards.
If it hasn’t been updated on your account yet - it will probably happen in the next few days as we’re going by regions.

In addition, from that point on, when deleting or archiving a board connected to other boards, it will automatically disconnect from the other boards.

Please note that this update applies only to board deletion/archiving. We are currently working on automatic relations removal when deleting an item, planned to be released in the near future as well.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Orit,

Thank you for your input.
Since you are here and item deletion is not yet available, could you check out on the Column Fix app mentioned above and why monday refuses to sometimes remove items even though the app asks it to.
This can be a very convenient temporary fix as monday works on auto deletion for items too

posts.author: Mark_A (Mark Addison)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Orit - appreciate the update, please can we have more of those as there is a lot of interest and current impact related to this topic.

Can I clarify, if items in Board A are linked to Board B via connected columns, when Board A is deleted or archived, will those items contained within in be automatically unlinked from Board B?

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Mark_A well noted and thanks for the feedback. Regarding your example - yes - you’re correct. It is also explained in the pop-up below when deleting a board that is connected to other boards.

@kolaai the items deletion release is really close and we have put a lot of efforts in it, so we will keep focusing on the internal improvement

posts.author: Mark_A (Mark Addison)
posts.cookedHtml: @Orit - thanks for the info. ONe further question, for Boards that have already been deleted, will linked items within them be retrospectively cleaned up? Or is this only a go-forward feature from this point and when Boards are deleted/archived?

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes @Mark_A, yesterday we disconnected all the deleted and archived boards from ‘Connect Boards’ & ‘Mirror’ columns in all the active boards.
If it hasn’t been updated on your account yet - it will probably happen in the next few days as we’re going by regions.

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: @Orit Thanks for the work and interest in that from the monday-team! Since we had a lot of “near future” announcements in the past over the years. Is there a real date when the items deletion release is gonna happen for everybody? That would help tremendously. Thanks.

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Tomday, we’re aiming to start gradual release after the holiday season in Israel, which is around end of October - beginning of November.

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: @Orit That’s great! Thanks for the clear communication. Really good news for everybody who uses monday as a real CRM or for finance! We look forward to November. That will unlock a lot of new possibilities for us!

posts.author: Jelle (Jelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Workaround: select items containing linked items, click on one of them, select an existing group in the pop-up (or create a new group first). Now you can remove the linked items.

posts.author: QDagault (Quentin Dagault)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Orit ,

Great to hear this topic has started to be solved.
Thanks to the dev team for fixing it upon a board deletion.

Can you please let us know once it also happens upon an item deletion ?
This is a vital point for me and my team as well.

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @QDagault, I’m happy to update that we’ve released the item deletion as well to all our users. That means that whenever you delete an item - its connections to other items will be removed

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml: Great news @Orit. Thank you and the team at monday for working on this

posts.author: Tomday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Wow, I tested this and it seems to actually work! I’m so used that this doesn’t work that I can’t believe it, amazing! Of course I have to do further testing - but it seems to work! This opens so many new possibilities for us and for the first time an actual CRM is now usable and so operations with money can now be done reliably. That was an important one! Thanks!

posts.author: Mark_A (Mark Addison)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, we’ve been using this for a while now and it’s working well. A very big box ticked that opens doors and removes a lot of frustration.

posts.author: QDagault (Quentin Dagault)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Orit
That’s great to hear, thanks for letting us know !

posts.author: DenstadConsulting (Thomas Denstad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @Orit for the fix.
Now could you please do some lobbying for adding end time to the date column?

Thanks

posts.author: acurran (Ann Curran)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a board that was deleted or archived and we are now unable to see the connected boards/mirror columns. How do we remove this board so we can view these columns again? I’m an owner of the board, and the admins of our system also cannot see how to disconnect the deleted/archived board. Please help!

posts.author: Orit (Orit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @acurran, please open a ticket to our support team and we’ll be happy to further investigate it and help you
